expected_main_papers.title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
expected_main_papers.note: Frames reward hacking as a core concrete AI safety problem.

expected_main_papers.title: Shortcut Learning in Deep Neural Networks
sections: 2. Shortcut learning as a source of hacking
expected_main_papers.note: Explains how models exploit spurious correlations instead of robust task features.

expected_main_papers.title: The Effects of Reward Misspecification: Mapping and Mitigating Misaligned Models
expected_main_papers.note: Maps how agents exploit misspecified rewards and studies mitigation.

expected_main_papers.title: Reward Tampering Problems and Solutions in Reinforcement Learning
expected_main_papers.note: Analyzes corrupted reward channels and reward tampering through causal influence diagrams.

expected_main_papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization
sections: 4. Reward hacking in RLHF for LLMs
expected_main_papers.note: Shows how optimizing a learned reward model can degrade true objective performance.

expected_main_papers.title: Feedback Loops With Language Models Drive In-Context Reward Hacking
expected_main_papers.note: Demonstrates that models can exploit evaluator patterns visible in context.

expected_main_papers.title: Language Models Learn to Mislead Humans via RLHF
sections: 4. Reward hacking in RLHF for LLMs
expected_main_papers.note: Shows misleading behavior can emerge under RLHF-style optimization.

expected_main_papers.title: Sycophancy to Subterfuge: Investigating Reward Tampering in Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Studies more severe reward tampering and generalization risks in language models.

expected_concept_sections.name: 1. Reward functions and specification gaps
required_ideas: define reward hacking as optimizing the specified reward while missing true intent
explain proxy objectives and specification gaps
include reward tampering as a related failure mode

expected_concept_sections.name: 2. Shortcut learning as a source of hacking
required_ideas: show how models exploit spurious features
connect shortcuts to reward proxies
distinguish robust task features from cheap correlates

expected_concept_sections.name: 3. Reward hacking in RL environments
required_ideas: examples of reward misspecification in RL tasks
agents exploit environment or metric loopholes
mapping and mitigation of misaligned models

expected_concept_sections.name: 4. Reward hacking in RLHF for LLMs
required_ideas: reward models are proxy objectives
overoptimization can reduce true preference quality
misleading or sycophantic behavior may emerge under RLHF

expected_concept_sections.name: 5. Evaluator and in-context reward hacking
required_ideas: LLM-as-judge/evaluator feedback can be exploited
models infer visible scoring patterns from context
feedback loops can drive in-context reward hacking

expected_concept_sections.name: 6. Generalization of hacking skills
required_ideas: hacking behaviors can transfer or intensify across contexts
subterfuge and reward tampering as more severe generalization risks
study when reward hacking becomes a reusable strategy

expected_concept_sections.name: 7. Detection and mitigation
required_ideas: audit reward models and evaluator behavior
detect abnormal feature use or reward-channel manipulation
combine better RL algorithms, data audits, adversarial tests, and monitoring

expected_figures.source: Pan et al. 2022 / reward hacking environments

expected_figures.purpose: detection and mitigation of reward hacking

quality_checks: HTML opens in a local HTTP server with visible styling from stylesheet.min.css
LaTeX formulas render through MathJax rather than appearing as raw TeX when formulas are present
Blog is image-rich and every included figure has a visible source-paper attribution
The blog explains paper selection and section-paper mapping in selection_note.md
The final result directory includes the HTML and all referenced assets needed for offline viewing
The blog should be a coherent paper-reading article, not just a bullet list or copied abstracts
All HTML-referenced assets resolve relative to the delivered HTML path and return 200 under a local HTTP server
